topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The spreadsheet glowed accusingly on my monitor. I scrolled down, my breath catching in my throat with every row that flashed past. Sarah’s salary, listed next to mine, was a figure that made my stomach clench. It wasn't just a difference; it was a chasm. My fingers hovered over the mouse, trembling slightly. Every click felt monumental, a step closer to a realization I wasn't prepared for. I quickly minimized the window, forcing a deep, shaky breath. I needed to process this, to figure out how I would even begin to handle the sheer imbalance of the situation. My palms were slick against the cool plastic of the keyboard.

[story 2]

"Wait. What?" The words felt thick and clumsy in my mouth. I stared at the quarterly reports, the numbers swimming before my eyes. Liam’s compensation package was significantly higher than mine. A cold knot tightened in my chest, spreading outwards like ice water. I gripped the edge of my desk so tightly my knuckles turned white. I wanted to look away, to pretend the screen was showing something else, but my gaze kept snapping back to those figures. My heart began to beat a frantic rhythm against my ribs, loud enough, I was sure, for anyone nearby to hear. I needed an escape route, a way to stop looking.

[story 3]

I reread the pay stub for the tenth time. The ink seemed to blur. It was impossible. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. A wave of nausea washed over me, making the room tilt slightly. My jaw felt locked, and I couldn't seem to articulate a coherent thought. This information felt like a physical blow, knocking the air from my lungs. I started pacing the small space between my desk and the filing cabinet, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. I kept running my hands through my hair, a desperate, futile attempt to ground myself against the sudden, overwhelming sense of dread flooding my system.
